# Backlog

*Generated by `/jim:backlog` — 2026-04-29*

---

### `/jim:complete` skill

Extract `/jim:build`'s completion gate (ARCHITECTURE.md refresh, BACKLOG.md regeneration, plan-status confirmation) into a dedicated, re-invokable skill. Today the gate runs only as the tail of a long build run, where attention is thinnest. A standalone `/jim:complete` enables re-running completion work after interrupted builds, manual commits, or post-hoc drift, and gives future completion concerns (changelog updates, doc-site regen, notifications) a clean home. Idempotency is a design constraint — running the skill twice must be safe (regenerate not append, merge not duplicate). Config adherence motivated the extraction; the architectural separation of concerns is the stronger long-term reason.

**Sources:** `docs/specs/jim/012-config-adherence/spec.md`, `docs/brainstorms/20260422-config-adherence.md`

### Tier-2 runtime exercise of jim skills under fixture-project

The runtime companion to `/jim:meta-test`'s static audit. Set up a fixture project with non-default `.jim/config.md` (e.g., `path.architecture: docs/jim/ARCHITECTURE.md`, `path.specs: docs/myproject/specs`) and a non-trivial subdirectory layout, then drive `/jim:spec → /jim:plan → /jim:build` end-to-end and assert that every artifact lands at the overridden path, every commit references the resolved value, and every Bash invocation honors the cd-safe placeholder expansion. Validates *behavior*, not just structural facts about source files. Distinct from `bin/jim_path`'s verify battery (which exercises the helper alone) and from `/jim:meta-test` (which audits markdown). Strongest signal for catching regressions at the integration layer.

**Sources:** `docs/specs/jim/014-meta-test/spec.md`, `docs/specs/jim/014-meta-test/plan.md`

### Anti-pattern lint for jim skills and agents

Extend or complement `/jim:meta-test` with structural and prose checks beyond config adherence. Specific candidates documented in `docs/specs/jim/001-meta/research.md` and the four anti-patterns listed in `ARCHITECTURE.md`'s Plugin Conventions: personality soup ("I am an AI assistant"), instruction shadowing (CLAUDE.md repeats), permission creep (Write/Bash in read-only agents), duplicate logic (same prose in 3+ agents), and structural checks like SKILL.md ≤ 500 lines, frontmatter field presence, file-reference existence. Could land as a fifth check inside `/jim:meta-test`, a sibling `/jim:meta-lint` skill, or a Vale-style external lint. Scope decision deferred until the first concrete drift surfaces.

**Sources:** `docs/specs/jim/014-meta-test/spec.md`, `docs/specs/jim/014-meta-test/plan.md`

### Schema-vs-skill cross-reference audit

Verify the bidirectional integrity of the schema-as-authority relationship: (a) every key declared in `skills/_shared/config-schema.md`'s `keys:` frontmatter has at least one consumer skill or agent that references it; (b) every `{path.*}` / `{specs.*}` / `{workflow.*}` placeholder used in skills or agents resolves to a key actually declared in the schema. Catches dead schema keys and typos in placeholder names. `/jim:meta-test`'s current four checks audit *value* leaks but not *key* lifecycle. `/jim:config` and `/jim:meta-skill` are the natural touch points for this concern, but a static audit is cheaper than waiting for a runtime resolution failure.

**Sources:** `docs/specs/jim/014-meta-test/spec.md`, `docs/specs/jim/014-meta-test/plan.md`

### CI integration for /jim:meta-test

Run the meta-test's four invariant checks on every PR via GitHub Actions or equivalent. The slash-command is in-conversation only by 014's design — relying on every contributor to remember `/jim:meta-test` before pushing is fragile. A parallel `bin/` script (e.g., `bin/jim_meta_test`) that exits non-zero on violations, paired with a CI workflow, makes the audit a hard gate. Pairs naturally with the existing `Shellcheck CI for executable artifacts` ad-hoc item — both are CI-discipline work for jim itself.

**Sources:** `docs/specs/jim/014-meta-test/spec.md`, `docs/specs/jim/014-meta-test/plan.md`

### Resolved-paths audit log

Persistent log file (e.g., `.jim/logs/resolved-paths.jsonl`) capturing each skill invocation's resolved-paths table for post-hoc debugging of which config was in effect when. Trivial to add once the resolve-paths preamble exists. Helps diagnose past drift — "which config produced this artifact?" becomes answerable from a file rather than reconstructed from the conversation log.

**Sources:** `docs/specs/jim/012-config-adherence/spec.md`, `docs/brainstorms/20260422-config-adherence.md`

### Cross-framework integration

Integration with other coding agents — Codex, Gemini CLI, and similar — so jim's SDLC discipline can travel beyond Claude Code. Currently jim is Claude-Code-only by design, but the pattern (specs, plans, research, agents) is portable. Larger investment than the other items; appropriate post-2.0 release.

**Sources:** `ROADMAP.md` (`## Later`)

### Research collaboration improvements

Refine the research workflow based on real-world usage feedback. Specifics emerge from actual user signals after the public release; held in backlog until concrete patterns surface.

**Sources:** `ROADMAP.md` (`## Later`)

## Ad-hoc

<!-- Items added here are preserved across /jim:backlog runs.     -->
<!-- Use `### Title` headings followed by a free-form description. -->
<!-- Add items manually or via `/jim:backlog add <description>`.   -->

### Meta-test: audit-surface symlink policy

Define meta-test behavior when the audit-surface Glob (`skills/*/SKILL.md`, `agents/*.md`) matches a symlink. Three failure modes need a documented response: (a) the audit reads content outside the intended surface (realpath escapes project root); (b) symlink loops or links to large files exhaust Claude's tool budget mid-audit; (c) a symlink targeting a clean SKILL.md masks a real violation by aliasing to it. Repo-review discipline is the primary defense in jim's tightly-controlled repo; meta-test should at minimum halt with an error if it encounters a symlink in the audit surface, mirroring `config-schema.md`'s realpath + project-root containment posture. Source: docs/specs/jim/014-meta-test/security.md finding 10.

### Meta-test verifies schema restricted-YAML format constraint

Meta-test should statically verify that `skills/_shared/config-schema.md` and any committed `.jim/config.md` conform to the restricted YAML subset documented in `config-schema.md`'s Schema Format Constraint section (single-line scalars, no anchors/aliases/merges, three-field key entries, etc.). The runtime defense in `bin/jim_path` already exits with `malformed schema` / `malformed config` if violated, so this is hardening rather than a requirements gap — surface the violation at PR time instead of first invocation. Source: docs/specs/jim/014-meta-test/security.md finding 7.

### Shellcheck CI for executable artifacts

`bin/jim_path` (introduced in spec 013) is jim's first non-markdown executable artifact. Add a CI workflow that runs `shellcheck bin/*` on every push to enforce sustained static-analysis discipline as the executable surface grows. Optional companion: a small test harness exercising each schema key against a fixture project — likely subsumed by the future Self-test meta-skill rather than built standalone. Source: docs/specs/jim/013-jim-path-helper/security.md finding 5.

### Read-only invariant check for hooks.pre-completion side effects

Pre-completion hooks (`hooks.pre-completion`, introduced in spec 015) run immediately before `/jim:arch` and `/jim:backlog` regenerate `ARCHITECTURE.md` and `BACKLOG.md`. A successful hook (exit 0) can side-effect project files between hook return and the doc-regen calls — e.g., modifying `plan.md` to mark unchecked tasks complete, or touching `ARCHITECTURE.md` to bias the architect's diff. Spec 015 accepts the trust model (defers to user shell), but a defensive invariant check would record file mtime or hash for `plan.md`, `ARCHITECTURE.md`, and `spec.md` before the hook runs and verify they're unchanged after. Out of scope for 015; track for follow-up if dogfooding surfaces an incident. Source: docs/specs/jim/015-build-hooks/security.md Finding 3.

### `/jim:backlog` removal path for resolved ad-hoc items

`/jim:backlog`'s regeneration mode re-emits ad-hoc items verbatim — the only automated removal path is step 9's duplicate detection (ad-hoc-vs-sourced match). Resolution-by-completion is NOT a removal trigger: when an ad-hoc item describes work that's since shipped (e.g., "Configurable build hooks" became spec 015 and was removed manually after build completion), regeneration preserves the stale entry. Documented workaround is manual `Edit` of `BACKLOG.md`. Two viable solutions: (a) add a `/jim:backlog remove <title>` argument analogous to `add`; (b) extend the resolution filter (step 6) to cover ad-hoc items, with explicit user confirmation per removal. (a) preserves user agency cleanest. Source: dogfood feedback during 015-build-hooks completion gate.

---

## Themes

### Config adherence — post-meta-test hardening

The 012/013/014 line shipped placeholder discipline, the `bin/jim_path` helper, and the static `/jim:meta-test` audit. What remains in this line is the runtime-side complement and the hardening of the meta-test against edge cases the v1 surface does not yet cover — symlink behavior, schema format constraints, and a fixture-project dogfood that exercises the full SDLC under non-default config. Each addresses a specific gap the placeholder syntax + helper + static audit together still leave open.

**Related items:** Tier-2 runtime exercise of jim skills under fixture-project, Resolved-paths audit log, Meta-test: audit-surface symlink policy, Meta-test verifies schema restricted-YAML format constraint

### Plugin static-analysis discipline

Continuous-enforcement work for jim itself, separate from the SDLC artifacts jim produces. CI gates that catch regressions at PR time instead of on next session, broader anti-pattern coverage beyond config adherence, and bidirectional schema-consumer integrity audits. Each item shifts a class of error from "discovered at runtime" to "blocked at commit."

**Related items:** Anti-pattern lint for jim skills and agents, Schema-vs-skill cross-reference audit, Shellcheck CI for executable artifacts, CI integration for /jim:meta-test

### Workflow extension

Deepening the SDLC surface beyond the current skills. Splitting `/jim:complete` out of `/jim:build` is the immediate move; refining research collaboration based on real-world signals is the longer-term one.

**Related items:** `/jim:complete` skill, Research collaboration improvements
